Empires & Puzzles Costume Lemminkäinen Review: A Swan Song or a Comeback?

Lemminkäinen is back with a fancy new costume, a Fighter class, and a slightly better attitude. But is this 'Swan Song' a chart-topper or just background noise? We break down whether the stat boost and revive talent are enough to make this Kalevala classic relevant again.
Costume Lemminkäinen: Putting Lipstick on a... Swan?

Lemminkäinen has returned from the river of Tuonela, this time sporting his "Swan" costume. Released in July 2025, this costume attempts to breathe new life into one of the Kalevala event's most underwhelming 4-stars. With a shift to the
class and updated stats, the question remains: is this enough to make him usable, or is this truly just his swan song?

The costume swaps his rugged barbarian look for something a bit more... refined? He looks noble, determined, and perhaps a bit tragic, fitting the "Fading Champion" moniker. The art is solid, capturing the mythological essence of the Kalevala legends. It’s a definite upgrade in terms of "vibes," making him look less like a generic warrior and more like a hero with a destiny.
The costume pushes his stats to
This is a noticeable improvement over his base stats (646/616).
Even with this boost, he is
behind the curve. Compare him to a modern 4-star like
(794 Attack). The gap is smaller, but it's still a canyon. He is playable now, but he's not a stat monster.
Special Skill: Sword of Swan Song
is a tweaked version of his original skill. Let's look at the trade-offs:
. This is a nice bump. hitting 300% on a target at Average speed is decent for a 4-star.
over 4 turns. This is a nerf. You lose about 20 damage total (5 damage per turn).

He hits slightly harder upfront but ticks for slightly less. Given how fast the meta moves, front-loading damage is usually preferred, so this is a net positive, albeit a small one. The "Anti-Ice" uncleansable burn mechanic remains, which is still niche.
Average. With the costume bonus (and potential mana troops), you can make him slightly faster, but he's not breaking any speed records.
, he has a ~30% chance to revive with 1 HP. Combined with his passive (recover health/mana on low HP), this synergy is the
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
This is where the Costume gets interesting.
The Kalevala bonus reduces damage from Ice specials. Again, great for a tank, weird for an attacker you shouldn't bring against Blue tanks.
This passive is the star of the show.
Lemminkäinen gets hit and drops to low HP.
If he dies, he might Revive.

If he revives, he is at "low health," potentially setting him up to trigger the passive again (if he hasn't already used it once per battle) or just fire his skill immediately with the mana gain. This makes him an annoying cockroach to kill.
A simple, boring, but effective boost. It helps his direct damage and his burn damage. It's fine, but less exciting than the Base version's Dodge.
The costume brings him from "Obsolete" to "usable in 4-star tournaments." He is no longer a liability, but he is certainly not a carry.
(Burn is good). In open wars, he is still outclassed by faster, harder-hitting reds.

. The ability to revive is game-changing for close matches. The 300% hit is also better for finishing off targets than the 285% hit, even with slightly less burn. The stats are strictly better. There is almost no reason to use the base version unless you desperately need the Dodge Aether Power or Barbarian bleed.
With the Fighter class, we can lean into his survivability or his damage.
Maximizes his 300% hit and burn. Fighters naturally revive, so making him hit harder when he
Makes him extremely annoying to kill. He stays alive to burn things and absorb hits.
. A 4-star damage dealer who doesn't deal damage is useless, no matter how many times they revive.
Fighter Revive + Sisu Passive (Heal/Mana) is a fantastic combo.
Still useful against pesky Blue bosses in events.
He isn't made of paper anymore (maybe cardboard now).
He is effectively a sniper who tickles the neighbors.
He competes with C.Colen (AOE nuke), C.Kelile (Fast Burn), and modern beasts.
Especially Rush (Average -> Fast) or Bloody Battle.
His Sisu passive helps sustain him through floors.
Use him as a "finisher" or a secondary threat.
A team of Fighters who refuse to die and keep burning the enemy.
If he can't fire, he's just a guy in a swan costume.
Doesn't affect him directly, but prevents him from gaining pseudo-HP.
The Costume makes him a solid 4-star for your early game. Level him! The Fighter emblems are valuable, but he is a good placeholder until you get Season 4/5/Construct heroes.
If you are drowning in Fighter emblems and have a soft spot for Kalevala, sure, max him. But realistically, he's staying on the bench. He's not better than your Toon boldtusk or C2 Colen.
"I ate him He has no place in any decent roster."
- A sentiment that even the costume struggles to fight.
"A costume would help Lemminkäinen I guess. Look at the first special..."
- Well, the costume is here. Did it help? A bit. But probably not enough for this player.
"Depends on your roster depth but for me I ate them both."
- The harsh reality of E&P rosters in 2026.

Costume Lemminkäinen is a definitive upgrade over his base form. The Fighter class breathes some utility into his kit, and the stat bump is welcome. However, "better than garbage" doesn't necessarily mean "good." He is a competent 4-star hero now, but in an era of gods and monsters, he's just a guy with a sword and a dream.
A playable, average hero who can be annoying to face but rarely fearsome.
Does the Swan Costume save Lemminkäinen from the bench? We review the Costume version's new Fighter class, updated stats, and special skill to see if he's finally worth the mats.
